description: This chart plots the variation of two microstructural parameters of nano-sized DO3 Fe3Al ordered phase in Fe-Al based ferritic low-density steels as a function of Al weight percentage (Wt% Al, x-axis). The left y-axis shows volume fraction of the ordered phase (unit: %, represented by black circles), while the right y-axis shows the diameter of the ordered phase (unit: nm, represented by black squares). As Al content increases from 5 wt% to 9 wt%, the volume fraction of Fe3Al phase increases almost linearly from ~0.4% to ~1.95%, and the phase diameter first fluctuates between ~2.6 nm and ~2.8 nm at 5-7 wt% Al, then rises significantly to ~3.6 nm at 9 wt% Al, with error bars indicating measurement uncertainty. The data are derived from Small Angle X-Ray Scattering (SAXS) characterization, which show good consistency with TEM measurement results of ordered phase size. This figure confirms the existence of nano-sized Fe3Al ordered precipitates in the studied alloys, and supports the conclusion that these ordered phases act as an additional strengthening contributor to the ferritic low-density steels.
